preview_text:
The model sees this as an almost even three-way contest, with Japan at a slight statistical edge of 34.4% to win, marginally ahead of the Netherlands at 34.1%. The draw probability stands at 31.6%, making the match highly competitive rather than one-sided.

The narrow spread across outcomes reflects a closely matched pairing. No result dominates the forecast; a Netherlands victory, a Japanese triumph, or a stalemate all register within a few percentage points of one another. This suggests the match is likely to be competitive and outcome-uncertain.

The market consensus—sportsbooks and Polymarket combined—substantially favors the Netherlands at roughly 47%, with Japan's win probability compressed to around 25%. This represents a meaningful divergence from the model's assessment, a gap of approximately 13 percentage points in Netherlands' favor and a similar margin for Japan in the opposite direction. The dashboard does not adjudicate which source is more accurate; the discrepancy is noted for context.
